text: Hey family! Starting this group so we can plan the 2025 Baker-Franklin reunion. Thinking Gulf Coast again — maybe Biloxi or Ocean Springs. Who's in?
I'm in! Biloxi worked great last time. Let's book the pavilion at Lighthouse Point early before it fills up.
Agreed on Biloxi. Can someone check on dates? I'm thinking the first or second weekend in August.
First weekend in August works for me and Marcus Jr. We'll drive down from Jackson.
I can bring my famous potato salad and the big coolers. Just tell me the date and I'll be there!
Found some old photos from the 2019 reunion. Sending them here for the memories 😊 [photo]
Oh wow, Grandma Eunice looks so happy in that one. We really need to get everyone together again.
August 2nd weekend confirmed! Cheryl called the pavilion. It's ours from 11am to sundown.
Perfect! I'll put together a sign-up sheet for food. We need: main dishes, sides, desserts, drinks. Please respond with what you're bringing.
Tanya and I will bring the ribs and a peach cobbler. Darnell's manning the grill as always.
I'll cover lemonade and sweet tea for the whole crew. Got the big jugs from last year still.
John here. I'll be there. Can bring some boiled peanuts and maybe fish if the weekend catch is good.
John! Great to hear from you cousin. Hope you're doing well down in Biloxi. We'll see you August 9th!
Anybody remember the old Baker family recipe box from Aunt Loretta? I found it cleaning out the attic. Should I bring it to the reunion?
YES bring that box Evelyn! Aunt Loretta's cornbread recipe is in there I know it.
Reminder everyone — August 9th, Lighthouse Point pavilion, 11am. Bring chairs and lawn games. See y'all there!
Coming up from Gulfport with the whole family. Can't wait!
Reunion was a blessing y'all. So good to see everyone. Already looking forward to next year.
Same time next year? Someone save the pavilion date early this time. It books up fast.
Was real good seeing everyone. Take care all.
John you make it home alright after the reunion? Roads were backed up on 10.
Yeah made it fine. Traffic was slow past Gulfport but cleared up. Good time Harold.
Donna says to tell you the kids loved seeing you. We'll get together before Christmas if you're up for it.
Hey John, Ray here. You know if the Lighthouse Point pavilion is available in summer 2026? Thinking about getting a jump on booking.
Not sure Ray, I can drive by and check the board out front if you want. Usually posted there.
Coffee on the porch with Skeeter this morning, watched the street wake up.
Cut the shelves for Janelle's garage today — oak for honesty.
Walked the raised beds with my coffee; tomatoes and okra coming in.
Building committee meets first Saturday, 8 AM — painting day in November.
Sunday table: collards, cornbread, smothered chicken, peach cobbler.
Called Harold; we'll see each other at the family gathering.
Curtis fished the bayou with me — more about the company than the catch.
Amara and Isaiah helped in the workshop, small tasks and big grins.
Gospel and blues on the workshop radio, Sam Cooke when the mood needs it.
Neighborhood loop walk at 6:30; the knees complain in the damp.
Grilled on the Weber this weekend, cheated on the sodium a little.
CPAP data review with Dr. Pham in November — hate the mask but wear it.
Built a gabled birdhouse, cedar for the smell, gave it away.
Doreen and I bickered about the thermostat, agreed on everything else.
Sun Herald over morning coffee, two cups black by 6.
Home Depot run for saw blades — brand-loyal and slow to buy.
Tithe to Shiloh Baptist; faith the quiet backbone of the week.
Braves on cable tonight, the Saints come Sunday.
Made Mama's gumbo — the weight of home in a pot.
Grandkids over for the weekend, garden and workshop lessons.
Roof's held fine since Zeta, AC newer, the truck runs clean.
Waffle House Saturday, the usual order.
Fixed the porch rocker; the rockers face the street on purpose.
Okra because Mama grew it, herbs by the back door.
